name: Error enum design
description: Model distinct parser failure modes with a typed Rust enum instead of stringly typed errors.

name: Display for errors
description: Implement human-readable formatting while keeping the error type programmatic.

name: Error trait
description: Mark a displayable error type as a standard Rust error.

name: Source errors
description: Preserve lower-level error values inside higher-level domain errors.

name: From for errors
description: Centralize conversion from a low-level error into a domain error.

name: TryFrom for parsing
description: Represent fallible text-to-domain conversion with TryFrom.

name: Basic behavior checks
description: Write focused examples that document success and failure behavior.

name: Struct field design
description: Choose owned field types for a small configuration object.

name: Default
description: Provide a sensible default state for a domain type.

name: Methods
description: Add small inherent methods that improve a type's calling ergonomics.

name: Option modeling
description: Represent optional configuration explicitly with Option.

name: Result validation
description: Return typed validation failures instead of boolean success flags.

name: Borrowing in APIs
description: Avoid unnecessary ownership in read-only helper functions.

name: Domain structs
description: Use named fields to model inventory data clearly.

name: filter_map
description: Filter and transform collection items in one iterator step.

name: fold
description: Accumulate values across a collection with an explicit reducer.

name: Iterator judgment
description: Choose between loops and iterator chains based on readability.

name: IntoIterator
description: Expose natural iteration for an owning wrapper type.

name: Borrowed sort comparisons
description: Sort slices by comparing borrowed fields when cloning a key would be wasteful.

name: API surface structs
description: Define a small owned type that represents a completed API request.

name: Builder setters
description: Use consuming setters to make small builder APIs chainable.

name: Builder default state
description: Create an empty builder with the standard Default trait.

name: Builder Result output
description: Return Result from builders so incomplete state becomes a typed, recoverable error.

name: TryFrom for request data
description: Convert a raw input type into a validated request with TryFrom.

name: Documentation examples
description: Write doc examples that show fallible APIs without unwrap or expect.

name: Borrowed fields
description: Use lifetimes to store borrowed string slices in a struct.

name: Lifetime-backed views
description: Model a view type that borrows a slice of borrowed entries.

name: Pattern matching
description: Map enum variants to behavior with a clear match expression.

name: Cow strings
description: Use Cow to support borrowed or owned string data.

name: Focused behavior checks
description: Write compact examples for important inclusion and exclusion behavior.

name: Private domain fields
description: Hide representation details so construction can preserve domain invariants.

name: TryFrom for domain validation
description: Use TryFrom to convert raw text into a validated domain value.

name: Typed validation errors
description: Name distinct validation failures with an enum instead of string errors.

name: Display for value objects
description: Format a domain value through its public representation.

name: Display and Error for validation errors
description: Make typed validation errors useful to humans and standard error APIs.

name: FromStr for string parsing
description: Expose string parsing through the standard FromStr trait while reusing TryFrom validation.

name: Money struct definition
description: Represent monetary amounts securely by grouping amounts and currencies together.

name: Currency enum
description: Represent supported currencies using enums to enforce type safety.

name: Money constructor and accessors
description: Keep Money fields private while exposing a narrow constructor and read-only accessors as an invariant boundary.

name: Checked money addition
description: Return typed errors for currency mismatches and arithmetic overflow instead of panicking.

name: Parsing decimal money strings
description: Convert decimal price strings into safe integer minor units with specific typed errors.

name: Displaying money values
description: Implement Display for all supported currency symbols and cents formatting.

name: NonZero ports
description: Leverage NonZeroU16 so Port values store only non-zero numbers after construction.

name: Validated Host value object
description: Wrap host strings in a validated domain type, using TryFrom<&str> to avoid raw string usage and unnecessary caller allocation.

name: Owned Host conversion reuse
description: Support TryFrom<String> by reusing borrowed Host validation and storing the original owned String on success.

name: Composite network endpoints
description: Group Host and Port values into an Endpoint with private fields and narrow accessors.

name: Endpoint local defaults
description: Implement Default for known local settings without unwrap, expect, or panic.

name: Formatting network endpoints
description: Format endpoints through their public accessors using Display.

name: Define a raw register-user DTO
description: An HTTP adapter receives partial, untrusted data. Start by representing that boundary data separately from the domain command.

name: Define a validated register command
description: The application layer should receive a command type, not raw optional DTO fields. Keep command fields private and expose read-only accessors.

name: Convert a DTO into a command with TryFrom
description: A raw DTO can be missing required fields. TryFrom gives the adapter a standard fallible conversion into the application command.

name: Trim and reject empty DTO fields
description: Boundary strings often contain whitespace. Normalize only after checking the DTO field exists, then reject empty normalized values explicitly.

name: Convert a bulk DTO with indexed errors
description: Batch adapter input needs useful error context. Preserve the row index when one nested DTO cannot become a command.

name: Convert a domain event into an outbound DTO
description: Outbound adapters also need explicit boundaries. A domain event can become a transport DTO through an infallible From implementation.

name: Wrap order lines in a collection type
description: A collection wrapper can own a Vec while keeping later invariants and iteration APIs under one type.

name: Expose borrowed iteration with iter
description: Callers often need to inspect a collection wrapper without consuming it. A borrowed iterator exposes a view without leaking fields.

name: Consume a wrapper with IntoIterator
description: When callers are done with a wrapper, IntoIterator should let them move owned values out naturally.

name: Iterate over borrowed collection wrappers
description: For loops over &OrderLines should borrow each line instead of consuming the wrapper.

name: Iterate mutably over collection wrappers
description: Some update operations should borrow the wrapper mutably and edit each line in place without exposing the Vec field.

name: Validate collection wrappers with TryFrom
description: An order without lines is invalid. TryFrom<Vec<OrderLine>> can enforce that invariant at the wrapper boundary.

name: Define ConfigLoadError variants
description: A configuration loader can fail for different reasons. Start with an enum that keeps those reasons programmatic.

name: Format ConfigLoadError for people
description: Typed errors are for programs; Display provides stable human-readable messages at the boundary.

name: Preserve I/O source errors
description: When file reading fails, the high-level config error should keep the original std::io::Error as its source.

name: Convert io::Error with From
description: A From implementation centralizes how low-level I/O errors become configuration errors and enables ? at call sites.

name: Wrap ParseIntError for port parsing
description: Parsing configuration values should keep lower-level parse details while returning the loader error type.

name: Propagate loader errors with ?
description: Once conversions exist, loader functions can stay small and let ? preserve the typed error boundary.

name: Define domain order errors
description: Domain errors should name business-rule failures without mentioning databases, HTTP, or UI details.

name: Define repository boundary errors
description: Repository failures are infrastructure boundary failures. Keep them distinct from domain rule failures.

name: Define use-case error variants
description: The application layer can compose domain and repository errors without leaking adapter response details.

name: Convert domain errors into use-case errors
description: From keeps application orchestration code focused by centralizing conversion from domain errors.

name: Classify retryable boundary errors
description: Not every failure should be retried. Classification helpers let application code make decisions without string matching.

name: Translate application errors to status codes
description: HTTP status codes belong at the adapter edge. Translate typed application errors there instead of putting HTTP concepts in domain code.

name: Keep register-user domain types pure
description: Domain types should not depend on HTTP, JSON, databases, or logging setup. Start with pure command and value-object types.

name: Define an application repository port
description: Application code depends on domain types and port traits, not concrete database clients.

name: Implement a use case over a port
description: A use case coordinates domain rules and ports. It should be generic over the port trait so infrastructure stays replaceable.

name: Translate adapter DTOs at the edge
description: Adapter DTOs should be converted into domain commands before use-case logic runs.

name: Implement an infrastructure repository
description: Infrastructure implements application ports. The application trait stays the dependency boundary.

name: Map a handler boundary without framework leakage
description: A handler adapter should translate request DTOs, call the use case, and return response data without pushing HTTP concepts into domain code.

name: Represent a structured log event
description: Structured logging starts with events that have names and levels instead of ad-hoc strings.

name: Attach request fields to log events
description: Useful logs carry fields such as request_id and attempt count so operators can filter and correlate events.

name: Redact secrets in formatted output
description: Logging helpers should make accidental secret exposure difficult. A wrapper can intentionally hide sensitive values when formatted.

name: Create events from a request span
description: A span carries context shared by multiple events. Request-scoped fields should not be rebuilt by every call site.

name: Log error kinds as fields
description: Logs should capture error category fields without relying on fragile message parsing.

name: Create logging events at the application boundary
description: Domain code should stay free of logging setup. Application boundaries can create events with meaningful fields.

name: Define a bounded Percentage type
description: A percentage should not be represented as any arbitrary integer throughout the codebase.

name: Write table-driven valid percentage tests
description: Several valid boundary cases should be easy to scan. A table-driven test keeps inputs and expected values together.

name: Write table-driven invalid percentage tests
description: Invalid cases deserve precise tests too. Table-driven tests make it clear which inputs should fail.

name: Test Display for Percentage
description: Formatting is public behavior. A small table keeps display expectations obvious.

name: Document Percentage with an executable example
description: A doc example should show normal usage and handle fallible APIs without unwrap or expect.

name: Name table-driven test cases
description: Named cases make failing table-driven tests easier to diagnose and easier for humans to scan.
